text.title: competitive-analysis Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for competitive-analysis as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Analyzing only direct competitors and ignoring aspirational examples from other industries.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Direct competitors may all share the same blind spot. Aspirational examples expose solutions that the competitive set has not discovered. Pattern transfers across industries often yield the highest-value insights.

failure-mode.description: Copying a competitor pattern without understanding why they made that choice.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The competitor may have shipped that pattern to fix a specific problem in their system that does not apply to yours. Copying without understanding context imports constraints along with the solution.

failure-mode.description: Running competitive analysis once and treating findings as permanent.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Competitor products change continuously. Analysis older than 6-12 months describes a market that no longer exists. Schedule regular check-ins: major annually, minor quarterly, triggered when a competitor ships a significant update.

failure-mode.description: Treating competitive analysis as a substitute for user research.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Competitors' patterns may also be wrong. Users tolerate them because there is no better alternative. Validate competitive patterns with your own users before adopting them as design requirements.

failure-mode.description: Over-interpreting surface patterns without validating causation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Competitors may have implemented a pattern for reasons unrelated to UX (regulatory constraint, technical limitation, legacy code). Surface observation cannot determine causation. Always include human review before committing to a recommendation based on competitor behavior.
